title: Panel config, lens-aware evaluator, and reconcile wiring
description: Add the opt-in evaluator.panel config (enabled/lenses/maxConcurrent, off by default) and make runAgentEvaluation lens-aware: when panel.enabled with >=2 lenses, fan out one judge call per lens (bounded by maxConcurrent) and reconcile() the per-lens EvalResults into a single canonical evaluator='panel' EvalResult; otherwise run the existing single judge call byte-identically. The combine logic, programmatic eval, retry loop, and EvaluationRunResult shape are untouched.

successCriteria.description: evaluator.panel exists in EvaluatorSectionSchema as an object { enabled: boolean default false, lenses: z.array(z.string()) default [], maxConcurrent: z.number().int().min(1) default 4 }, mirrored in createDefaultConfig; a schema unit test asserts the defaults and that a config without a panel field still parses (panel resolves to the default).

With panel unset OR enabled=false OR lenses.length<2, runAgentEvaluation makes EXACTLY ONE judge LLM call and returns an EvalResult equivalent to today's single-judge path. A unit test mocks createClient (no real LLM) and asserts the client is invoked exactly once on the off path.

With panel.enabled=true and >=2 lenses, runAgentEvaluation issues one judge call per lens, never exceeding maxConcurrent concurrent calls, and returns reconcile(contractId, round, lensResults, timestamp) — a canonical EvalResult with evaluator='panel'. A unit test with a mocked client asserts call-count == lenses.length and peak concurrency <= maxConcurrent.

A 2-pass / 2-fail lens panel reconciles to passed=false (fail-closed), and the reconciled agentResult flows unchanged into runEvaluatorAgent's combine (passed = programmaticEval.passed && agentResult.passed). A unit test drives the mocked client to return 2 pass + 2 fail lens verdicts and asserts the final EvaluationRunResult.passed is false.
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, and npx eslint src/ exit 0; the full vitest suite stays green with no new failures beyond the documented flaky tool-count baseline.

nonGoals: Do not add the built-in lens prompt catalog or per-lens telemetry — that is sprint 2 (a single inline lens-focus string is enough this sprint).
Do not change the programmatic evaluators, the combine logic, the retry loop, or the EvaluationRunResult shape.
Do not modify src/orchestrator/workflow/reconciler.ts — reuse reconcile() as-is.
Do not touch the workflow engine, .claude/workflows, agents/*.md, or providers/types.ts.
Do not invoke any real LLM in tests — mock createClient.
stopConditions: Stop when the off path is a single judge call, the on path fans out + reconciles with bounded concurrency, and typecheck/build/lint/tests are green.

Stop when changes are confined to src/config/schema.ts (+ defaults.ts if its literal needs the field), src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts, and a new evaluator-agent.test.ts (plus schema.test.ts additions).
definitionOfDone: evaluator.panel is a validated opt-in config defaulting to off; runAgentEvaluation runs the existing single judge call when off (byte-identical, proven by a client-mocked single-call test) and fans out >=2 lenses bounded by maxConcurrent then reconcile()s into one evaluator='panel' EvalResult when on; a 2-2 tie fails closed and flows through the unchanged combine; typecheck/build/lint and the full suite are green.
assumptions: runAgentEvaluation (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts:134) builds the judge prompt and calls createClient; a lens is injected as a focus block into that prompt.
reconcile's sprintId/round args do not affect output, so contract.contractId and an iteration/round number (or 1) are safe to pass.
createClient can be mocked via vi.mock to avoid real LLM calls; the handoff carries the contract + iteration.
evaluatorNotes: Confirm EvaluatorSectionSchema.panel defaults (enabled false, lenses [], maxConcurrent 4) and that a config without panel parses. Read runAgentEvaluation: confirm the off path (panel off / <2 lenses) calls the judge exactly once and the lens-less prompt is unchanged from before (diff the prompt assembly — no lens text when lens is undefined). Confirm the on path maps lenses → runSingleLensEval and calls reconcile() from src/orchestrator/workflow/reconciler.ts (not a reimplementation). Run the new evaluator-agent.test.ts: verify the single-call off-path assertion, the call-count==lenses.length on-path assertion, the peak-concurrency<=maxConcurrent assertion (inspect how the mock counts concurrency), and the 2-2 tie → EvaluationRunResult.passed=false assertion. Confirm createClient is mocked (no real LLM/network). Run npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, npx eslint src/ (exit 0), and the full npx vitest run (tolerate only the flaky tool-count baseline). Confirm git diff is confined to schema.ts/defaults.ts, evaluator-agent.ts, evaluator-agent.test.ts, schema.test.ts; reconciler.ts and the combine logic unchanged.
